purpose: Ground truth for testing contract keyword/provision extraction and evidence text matching.
text_normalization: Replace non-breaking spaces and zero-width characters
Normalize Unicode dash variants to hyphen
Collapse repeated whitespace into a single space

provisions.text: Contract No.: EMP-2026-0720-001 | Effective Date: July 20, 2026 EMPLOYER EMPLOYEE Company: NovaTech Solutions Limited Business Reg. No.: 0319876543 Address: 12th Floor, Lotus Business Center, 45 Nguyen Hue Street, Ben Nghe Ward, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam Representative: Mr. David Nguyen, Chief Executive Officer Email: hr@novatechsolutions.example.com Phone: +84 28 3999 8899 Full Name: Emily Tran Date of Birth: March 15, 1998 ID/Passport No.: 079198004567 Permanent Address: 25 Tran Hung Dao Street, Phu Nhuan District, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam Email: emily.tran98@example.com Phone: +84 912 345 678 The Employer and the Employee are collectively referred to as the Parties. The Parties agree to enter into this Employment Agreement under the following terms and conditions.

ARTICLE 1. POSITION AND DUTIES The Employer employs the Employee as AI Software Engineer in the Technology Department. The Employee shall develop and maintain software applications, APIs, AI-related features, data workflows, automation tools, tests, and technical documentation; participate in code review and debugging; cooperate with cross-functional teams; comply with security and development procedures; and perform other reasonable duties consistent with the position. The Employee reports to Mr. Michael Le, Head of Technology Department, or another person designated by the Employer.

ARTICLE 2. TERM AND PROBATION This Agreement starts on August 1, 2026 and ends on July 31, 2028, unless renewed or terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement and applicable law. The probation period is two (2) months, from August 1, 2026 to September 30, 2026. During probation, the Employee receives 85% of the official gross salary, equal to VND 25,500,000 per month. Upon successful completion, official employment begins on October 1, 2026.

ARTICLE 3. WORKING LOCATION AND HOURS The primary workplace is NovaTech Solutions Limited, 12th Floor, Lotus Business Center, 45 Nguyen Hue Street, Ben Nghe Ward, District 1, Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. Depending on business needs, the Employer may reasonably require work at another office, client site, or remote location. Normal working hours are Monday to Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM, including a one-hour lunch break. Approved overtime shall be compensated in accordance with applicable labor laws and Company policy.

ARTICLE 4. SALARY, PAYMENT, AND BENEFITS The official gross monthly salary is VND 30,000,000 (Thirty million Vietnamese Dong). Salary shall be paid by bank transfer no later than the 5th day of the following month to: Account Holder: Emily Tran; Account No.: 190345678901; Bank: Techcombank, Ho Chi Minh City Branch. Statutory social insurance, health insurance, unemployment insurance, personal income tax, and other deductions shall be handled in accordance with Vietnamese law. Benefits may include annual leave, public holidays, performance bonus subject to evaluation, work equipment, training, health check-up, and other Company benefits.

ARTICLE 5. PERFORMANCE AND CONDUCT The Employee shall perform duties honestly, diligently, professionally, and with reasonable skill. The Employee shall comply with all internal rules on attendance, workplace behavior, information security, data protection, software usage, conflict of interest, and client confidentiality. The Employee shall not engage in any activity that harms the Company, its clients, partners, employees, reputation, business interests, or confidential information.

ARTICLE 6. CONFIDENTIALITY During and after employment, the Employee shall keep confidential all non-public information obtained through work, including source code, algorithms, system architecture, databases, product plans, client information, pricing, contracts, financial data, personnel records, internal reports, policies, and any information reasonably understood as confidential. Such information may not be disclosed, copied, transferred, published, or used for non-Company purposes without prior written approval.

ARTICLE 7. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY All inventions, source code, software, models, data pipelines, technical designs, documents, reports, workflows, and other work products created by the Employee within the scope of employment or using Company resources shall belong exclusively to the Company. The Employee assigns all rights, title, and interest in such work products to the Company and shall not reuse or distribute them without written approval. The Employee shall not knowingly use unauthorized third-party intellectual property or open-source software in Company projects without proper approval.

ARTICLE 8. COMPANY PROPERTY AND ACCESS The Company may provide the Employee with a laptop, software accounts, email account, source-code repository access, documents, security credentials, and other work tools. All Company property remains owned by the Company. Upon termination or upon request, the Employee shall return all Company property, revoke or transfer access as instructed, and delete Company information from personal devices unless the Company instructs otherwise.

ARTICLE 9. LEAVE AND ABSENCE The Employee is entitled to annual leave, public holidays, sick leave, and other leave benefits in accordance with applicable labor laws and Company policy. Leave requests must be submitted in advance and approved by the Employee’s manager, except in emergencies. Unauthorized absence may result in disciplinary action.

ARTICLE 10. TERMINATION AND HANDOVER This Agreement may be terminated upon expiry, mutual written agreement, resignation with lawful notice, termination by the Company as permitted by law, serious breach by either Party, or other lawful grounds. After probation, if the Employee resigns, the Employee shall provide at least thirty (30) days prior written notice unless the law requires otherwise. Upon termination, the Company shall pay outstanding salary and lawful benefits, subject to deductions and completion of handover. The Employee shall complete assigned handover tasks and cooperate to ensure a smooth transition.

ARTICLE 11. NON-SOLICITATION During employment and for twelve (12) months after termination, the Employee shall not directly solicit the Company’s clients, employees, contractors, or business partners for purposes that may harm the Company’s legitimate business interests. This clause shall apply only to the extent permitted by applicable law.

ARTICLE 12. DISCIPLINARY ACTION The Company may take lawful disciplinary action for violations of this Agreement, internal regulations, security requirements, confidentiality obligations, or applicable law. Disciplinary action may include verbal warning, written warning, suspension, termination, or other lawful measures depending on the seriousness of the violation and the applicable procedure.

ARTICLE 13. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION This Agreement is governed by the laws of Vietnam. Any dispute arising from or related to this Agreement shall first be resolved through good-faith negotiation. If no settlement is reached within thirty (30) days from written notice of dispute, either Party may submit the dispute to a competent court in Vietnam.

ARTICLE 14. ENTIRE AGREEMENT This Agreement constitutes the entire employment agreement between the Parties and supersedes all prior discussions, offers, or arrangements relating to the Employee’s employment. Any amendment must be made in writing and signed by both Parties. If any provision is invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain valid. This Agreement is made in two (2) original copies of equal legal validity; each Party keeps one original copy. The Parties confirm that they have read, understood, and voluntarily agreed to all terms and conditions of this Agreement. FOR AND ON BEHALF OF THE COMPANY NovaTech Solutions Limited EMPLOYEE Signature: ___________________________ Name: David Nguyen Title: Chief Executive Officer Date: July 20, 2026 Signature: ___________________________ Name: Emily Tran Date: July 20, 2026
